independentCandidates.currentSnippet:
SKILLS_GUIDANCE = (
    "After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, "
    "or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a "
    "skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.\n"
    "When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, "
    "patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. "
    "Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities."
)
independentCandidates.proposedReplacement:
SKILLS_GUIDANCE = (
    "After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, "
    "or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a "
    "skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.\n"
    "When creating a new skill, first check whether an installed skill named "
    "`skill-creator` is available in the skills index. If so, load it with "
    "skill_view(name='skill-creator') and follow its authoring and validation "
    "instructions before drafting the new SKILL.md. Then persist the final "
    "skill with skill_manage(action='create'). If `skill-creator` is not "
    "installed, fall back to the built-in class-level skill rules and continue; "
    "do not auto-install or fetch it. Do not force `skill-creator` for small "
    "targeted patches — use it for new skills and optionally for major full "
    "rewrites.\n"
    "When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, "
    "patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. "
    "Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities."
)
independentCandidates.reasoning: This is the primary foreground-agent constant that instructs the model to create skills via skill_manage. It is the canonical site for inserting the opt-in 'consult skill-creator if installed, otherwise continue' rule. The replacement preserves the existing patch-without-asking directive and gates only NEW skill creation on skill-creator.

)

reconciledFinalSet.finalReplacement: Insert the new-skill redirect into SKILLS_GUIDANCE: when a new skill is warranted, check the available-skills index; if `skill-creator` is installed, load it with skill_view(name='skill-creator') and follow its authoring/validation guidance; then persist with skill_manage(action='create'). If absent, fall back to built-in class-level rules; never auto-install or fetch. Do NOT force skill-creator for small targeted patches — reserve it for new skills and major full rewrites. Preserve the existing patch-instruction line ('patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch')').
reconciledFinalSet.decisionRationale: Both independent sweep and doc §1.A agree exactly on this site. It is the primary foreground-agent instruction that tells the model to create skills via skill_manage. Synchronizing this constant is the highest-leverage change because it is injected via tool_guidance for every agent run that has skill_manage available.

If absent, continue with built-in class-level rules; absence must not block creation; never auto-install.
reconciledFinalSet.decisionRationale: Independent sweep and doc §1.B agree. MEMORY_GUIDANCE is a second foreground surface that already nudges the agent toward creating a skill on discovery; without the redirect here, an agent reading both prompts would receive contradictory cues.

evidence: Independent sweep (read-only) of /Users/kiscsicska/.hermes/hermes-agent at commit 368fcf1ff (v0.16.0) used grep -rn with patterns 'create.*skill', 'skill_manage', 'SKILLS_GUIDANCE', 'MEMORY_GUIDANCE', 'build_skills_system_prompt', '_SKILL_REVIEW_PROMPT', '_COMBINED_REVIEW_PROMPT', 'spawn_background_review_thread' across the entire install (not just agent/). Each match's surrounding context was read to determine whether it (a) ships into a foreground agent's system prompt or (b) drives a forked background review that itself creates skills. Seven prompt-text anchors qualified: SKILLS_GUIDANCE (prompt_builder.py:172), MEMORY_GUIDANCE (prompt_builder.py:143), build_skills_system_prompt result string (prompt_builder.py:~1373), _SKILL_REVIEW_PROMPT (background_review.py:45), _COMBINED_REVIEW_PROMPT (background_review.py:150), system_prompt.py tool_guidance assembly (~117), and the skill_manage schema description (skill_manager_tool.py:~992). Sites that were inspected but REJECTED as patch points: agent/curator.py CURATOR_REVIEW_PROMPT and CURATOR_DRY_RUN_BANNER (operator-triggered consolidation pass, not the automatic background-review path; the doc scopes the change to _SKILL_REVIEW_PROMPT / _COMBINED_REVIEW_PROMPT only), agent/curator.py:633 inline f-string (interpolated log/diagnostic line, not a prompt template), tools/skill_manager_tool.py:1042 'Required for create and edit' (argument-validation prose, not a behavior-shaping prompt), tools/skills_guard.py and tools/skill_provenance.py (storage/provenance layer, not prompt text), web_server.py create_skill (HTTP dashboard endpoint, not the agent prompt surface), and all __tests__/*.ts and ui-tui files (test fixtures, not shipped prompts). doc/maybe-patch-points.md (read after the independent sweep) names exactly six patch points across four files: prompt_builder.py §1.A/§1.B/§1.C (SKILLS_GUIDANCE, MEMORY_GUIDANCE, build_skills_system_prompt), background_review.py §2.A/§2.B/§2.C (_SKILL_REVIEW_PROMPT, _COMBINED_REVIEW_PROMPT, do-not-change-spawn_background_review_thread), tools/skill_manager_tool.py §3 (optional schema-description clarification), and website/docs/user-guide/features/skills.md §4 (Agent-Managed Skills). Reconciliation: five sites are sourceOfTruth=both (full agreement between sweep and doc): SKILLS_GUIDANCE, MEMORY_GUIDANCE, build_skills_system_prompt result string, _SKILL_REVIEW_PROMPT option (4), _COMBINED_REVIEW_PROMPT option (4). One site is sourceOfTruth=merged: system_prompt.py tool_guidance assembly (sweep-only, no text change — comment-only edit to pin the synchronization contract). Two sites are sourceOfTruth=doc: skill_manage schema-description clarification (doc §3, optional) and website/docs/user-guide/features/skills.md (doc §4, documentation-only). Net final set: eight reconciled patch points across four files plus one docs file, all read-only-verified, no patches executed.
